# Sidebar

Builds responsive application navigation from composable layout and menu primitives.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Side, surface, and collapse behavior](#side-surface-and-collapse-behavior)
- [Menus, groups, and actions](#menus-groups-and-actions)
- [Open state and responsive behavior](#open-state-and-responsive-behavior)
- [Loading and empty groups](#loading-and-empty-groups)
- [Accessibility and composition](#accessibility-and-composition)

## Installation

Add the Sidebar component and its supporting primitives from the registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/sidebar
```

## Usage

Wrap the sidebar and main content in `SidebarProvider`. Compose header, scrollable content, and footer inside `Sidebar`, then use `SidebarInset` for the adjacent page surface. Use Sidebar for persistent application navigation or tools. Prefer Navigation Menu for a compact site header and a Sheet for temporary, unrelated content.

**Sidebar menu example**

```vue
<template>
  <SidebarProvider class="h-72 min-h-0 overflow-hidden rounded-lg border">
    <div class="w-64 bg-sidebar p-3 text-sidebar-foreground">
      <SidebarGroup>
        <SidebarGroupLabel>Workspace</SidebarGroupLabel>
        <SidebarGroupContent>
          <SidebarMenu>
            <SidebarMenuItem v-for="item in items" :key="item.label">
              <SidebarMenuButton as-child :is-active="item.active">
                <Link :href="item.href">
                  <component :is="item.icon" />
                  <span>{{ item.label }}</span>
                </Link>
              </SidebarMenuButton>
            </SidebarMenuItem>
          </SidebarMenu>
        </SidebarGroupContent>
      </SidebarGroup>
    </div>
    <div class="flex flex-1 items-center justify-center bg-background text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      Main content
    </div>
  </SidebarProvider>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { FileTextIcon, LayoutDashboardIcon, SettingsIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { Link } from '@inertiajs/vue3'
import {
  SidebarGroup,
  SidebarGroupContent,
  SidebarGroupLabel,
  SidebarMenu,
  SidebarMenuButton,
  SidebarMenuItem,
  SidebarProvider,
} from '@/Components/Sidebar'

const items = [
  { label: 'Overview', href: '/docs', icon: LayoutDashboardIcon, active: true },
  { label: 'Documents', href: '/docs/components/card', icon: FileTextIcon, active: false },
  { label: 'Settings', href: '/docs/installation', icon: SettingsIcon, active: false },
]
</script>
```

```vue
<SidebarProvider>
  <Sidebar collapsible="icon">
    <SidebarHeader>...</SidebarHeader>
    <SidebarContent>...</SidebarContent>
    <SidebarFooter>...</SidebarFooter>
    <SidebarRail />
  </Sidebar>
  <SidebarInset>
    <SidebarTrigger />
    <main>...</main>
  </SidebarInset>
</SidebarProvider>
```

## Side, surface, and collapse behavior

Set `side` to `left` or `right`. The `sidebar` variant sits against the viewport edge, `floating` adds an inset bordered surface, and `inset` visually pairs with `SidebarInset`.

Choose `collapsible="offcanvas"` when the whole sidebar may leave the desktop viewport, `icon` when primary destinations remain recognizable in a compact rail, and `none` when it must stay visible. Icon collapse is appropriate only when every item has a clear icon and a tooltip or other accessible label.

```vue
<Sidebar side="left" variant="inset" collapsible="icon">...</Sidebar>
```

## Menus, groups, and actions

Put related destinations in `SidebarGroup`, describe the group with `SidebarGroupLabel`, and render rows through `SidebarMenu` and `SidebarMenuItem`. Mark the current destination with `is-active`; do not use active styling for hover, focus, or an expanded submenu.

`SidebarMenuButton` defaults to a button. Compose an Inertia `Link` with `as-child` for navigation. Use `SidebarMenuAction` for a secondary action associated with the row, and give icon-only actions an accessible name. Badges should convey compact status or counts, not duplicate the item label.

```vue
<SidebarMenuButton as-child :is-active="isActive">
  <Link href="/projects">
    <FolderIcon />
    <span>Projects</span>
  </Link>
</SidebarMenuButton>
```

Use submenus for a shallow destination hierarchy. Compose Collapsible around a group or menu item when users benefit from hiding its children; keep frequently used destinations directly reachable.

## Open state and responsive behavior

`SidebarProvider` owns separate desktop and mobile state. It uses a Sheet on mobile, persists desktop open state in the sidebar cookie, and supports Command+B or Control+B. Include `SidebarTrigger` wherever users must be able to reopen it; `SidebarRail` is an additional pointer target, not a replacement for the trigger.

Use `default-open` for initial uncontrolled state or `v-model:open` when the surrounding layout must coordinate the desktop state. Call `useSidebar()` only inside the provider to access `state`, mobile state, and `toggleSidebar`.

```vue
<SidebarProvider v-model:open="sidebarOpen">
  <Sidebar />
  <SidebarInset>...</SidebarInset>
</SidebarProvider>
```

## Loading and empty groups

Use `SidebarMenuSkeleton` only while an existing menu structure is loading, and keep the number of placeholders close to the expected result. Remove empty groups rather than rendering an unexplained label. Do not keep navigation in a permanent loading state; provide a visible failure and recovery path.

## Accessibility and composition

Navigation rows must keep link semantics, while row actions must remain buttons. Visible labels should remain available to assistive technology when icon collapse hides them. Preserve focus when collapsing; if the focused item becomes unavailable, move focus to the trigger deliberately.

Components such as menu buttons, group labels, and actions support `as` or `as-child`. Custom children must forward attributes, events, and refs as described in the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition).

Hosted documentation: [Sidebar](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/sidebar)
